column1: ### Data collection and cleaning
Trawl biosample data collected using trawling method
column2: with net size usually of 2x2x7.5m
column3: in water bodies located in British Columbia
column4: Canada
column5: from 1977 to 1999 by researchers from Fisheries and Oceans Canada as part of the Lake Enrichment Program (LEP). Synthetic columns were created during data rescue and validation. Raw columns were recovered from the original files.

column1: Note that both *.dat files and SAS files were used as input for this data recovery and data cleaning effort by Alice Assmar in 2025-2026. The *.dat files in 1977-1983 (and some 1984) are in a different format than the other *.dat files
column2: and original data appears to have been overwritten in this other format. Therefore
column3: SAS files were used in those years. These SAS files were originally derived from the original *.dat files
column4: which have since been lost for those years.

column1: After 1983
column2: and until 1999
column3: both format of files were available
column4: and where there were conflicts between the two

column1: The *.dat files did not have header names as part of the data; these were added when the data were read into R
column2: and an intermediate file *.csv file was created;

column1: The trawl biosampling data after 1999
column2: and the acoustic target data after 2007
column3: were sometimes available in Excel files or scanned field notes held by the Salmon in Regional Ecosystems Program at Fisheries and Oceans Canada (contact Patrick.Thomspon@dfo-mpo.gc.ca)
column4: but the data in those Excel and scanned longhand files have not been extracted as part of the current data recovery and cleaning process.

column1: The output files are saved as *.csv files. We refer to “columns”
column2: “headers”
column3: and “rows” below for simplicity
column4: in reference to the *.csv when it is converted to a table
column6: the way that it opens in Excel.

column1: ### Description of variables
ats_year: Trawl biosample year in which the data were collected (“ats” stands for Acoustic Trawl Survey). The ats_year runs from April 1 to March 31 of the following calendar year. Thus
column2: ats_year is equal to calendar year for survey dates from April 1 to Dec 31
column3: and equal to calendar year minus one for dates from Jan 1 to March 31. This is the year that should be used to group data for annual analysis as it corresponds to a single cohort of age-0+ juvenile Sockeye
column4: which emerges from eggs
column5: moves into the nursery lake after April 1
column6: and leaves the lake as smolts the following year before the end of March. Some juvenile Sockeye (age-1+) stay an extra year in the lake and leave as smolts the following year before the end of March. Note that the March 31/April 1 cut-off may not apply to all Sockeye nursery lakes; the analyst should review published data
column7: and technical and manuscript reports in the Canadian Reports of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences series (available at DFO libraries) to determine these dates for individual lakes of interest. Grouping by calendar year would result in fish being misassigned to cohorts. Synthetic column.

column1: lake_code: Numeric code identifying the sampled lake (or a section or arm of a lake) in the original source files. Used to join lake name and geographic coordinate information via a lookup table (LDP_ATS_Rescue/TRAWL_BIOSAMPLE/00_raw_data/04_YS_look_up_tables/lake_codes.csv). lake_name
column2: rather than lake_code
column3: should be used for modern analysis. The lake_code can be used as it is a unique identifier
column4: though arbitrary
column5: originally devised by the LEP program. Raw column.

column1: lake_name: Name of the lake where the sample was collected. Synthetic column created using the lake_code and a lookup table (LDP_ATS_Rescue/TRAWL_BIOSAMPLE/00_raw_data/04_YS_look_up_tables/lake_codes.csv). Note that lake No-Name (lake_code 124)
column2: from the original 1988 *.dat and SAS files
column3: was replaced with the true lake name and code (Link Lake; lake_code 180) in the look-up table.

column1: trawl_date: Trawl sampling date converted to ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) from a variety of original formats in the original dataset. Raw column.
trawl_month: Month when the trawl biosample data was collected. Synthetic column extracted from trawl_date for use in grouping and organizing data.

trawl_number: Trawl record number. This is a number occurring (usually) sequentially within a set of trawls conducted together (sometimes over two or more days) that identifies an individual trawl set event. Sometimes individual trawl numbers are skipped in the sequence in the original data. The trawl_number ranges from 1-39; i.e. the maximum number of trawls that were conducted together in this dataset was 39. Raw column.

gear_info: Dimensions of the net used in the sampling event. This field can also provide gear information. This data is largely unstandardized and should be tidied/standardized before use. Raw column.
gear_type: Specifies which gear type was used for a sample
column2: derived from the trawl_comment (originally called trawl_location). If the search term “sein*” was found in the trawl_comment column
column3: the gear_type was listed as “Beach seine”. See trawl_comment below; it is defined under the general_comments entry. "Trawl" was entered for all other gear_type records. Synthetic column.

column1: depth_m: Depth of the trawl sampling in meters. This variable represents the depth of the individual trawl event. The value range is 0–70 meters
column2: with most observations between 0-20 meters. Two records showed values "188" (Henderson lake) and "200" (Owikeno Lake)
column3: which were likely typos. They were replaced by "18" and "20
column4: respectively, and these changes were flagged as depth_m_comments"
column5: within the "general_comments" column. The distribution of depth data by lake can be found in the Tables and plots section. Raw column.

column1: orig_start_time: Standardized start time of the trawl sampling rescued from SAS and *.dat files. The format is "HH:MM:SS". All values are local time in Pacific Time (UTC−08 or UTC-07 depending on year and time of year). Records with invalid formats were corrected when possible
column2: or replaced by NAs
column3: and these issues are flagged in the "data_issues" column as "invalid_start_time". Raw column
column4: with flagging of any changes that were made to the raw data.

column1: orig_end_time: Standardized end time of the trawl sampling rescued from SAS and *.dat files. The format is "HH:MM:SS". All values are local time in Pacific Time (UTC−08 or UTC-07 depending on year and time of year). Records with invalid formats were corrected
column2: when possible
column3: or replaced by NAs
column4: and these issues are flagged in the "data_issues" column as invalid_end_time. Raw column
column5: with flagging of any changes that were made to the raw data.

column1: calc_end_time: Standardized calculated end time of the trawl sampling. This column contains end times calculated from the provided orig_start_time and orig_duration_minutes. The format is "HH:MM:SS". All values are local time in Pacific Time (UTC−08 or UTC-07 depending on year and time of year). In total
column3: 921 calculated end times were estimated when no orig_end_time was in the dataset. These modifications were flagged in the “data_issues” column as calc_end_time_comment. Synthetic column.

column1: orig_duration_minutes: Duration of the trawl sampling in minutes. This variable represents the duration of each trawl event. The expected value range is 0–60 minutes
column2: with most observations near 15 minutes. Values exceeding 60 minutes were flagged in the invalid_duration_time section of the data_issues column as “Greater than 60 min
column3: likely error”. Extreme values (e.g. 365 and 535) were set to NA. Raw column
column4: with flagging of any changes that were made to the raw data.

column1: calc_duration_time: Standardized calculated duration time of the trawl sampling. This column contains duration times calculated from the start time and end time in the original dataset. Times are reported in minutes. In total
column3: 516 entries do not match original duration (orig_duration_minutes)
column4: likely error in orig_end_time column
column5: and 0 missing duration-time values were replaced with calculated duration times. Mismatches are flagged in the duration_comment section of the data_issues column. Synthetic column.

column1: fish_class_count: Integer identifying an individual fish within a sample record (formerly called fish_id)
column2: based on its order of appearance
column3: and often based on its category
column4: as in the example from Kennedy Lake (Clay) on April 3

column1: The fish_class_count resets for each fish species and life-stage combination within a single trawl. So Sockeye fry
column2: Stickleback fry
column3: Stickleback Subadult and Stickleback Adult can all have a fish_class_count=1 because they are different species and life stages. The fish_class_count number also resets again for a new trawl. In other words
column4: the numbering restarts for each combination of trawl_number
column6: and life_stage for a given lake_name and trawl_date
column7: and ranges from 1 to fish_class_total for that combination of species_common_name

column1: species_code: Standardized LEP code identifying the fish species. This is a synthetic column that was added to the look-up table
column2: AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv
column3: and which is used to collapse the different life stages x species into the same species for analysis. E.g.
column4: “Sockeye (juv)” and “Sockeye (fry)” were originally given different species_info_codes in the original data but in the synthetic species_code column in the look-up table
column5: they have the same species_code because both are Sockeye.

column1: species_common_name: Widely recognized
column2: non-scientific name to identify the fish species. Based on the AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv table. Synthetic column.

column1: life_stage: Life-cycle stage of the fish at the time of collection. Synthetically derived from the species_info_code (formerly the LEP “species code”) column. Uses look-up table: AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv. Synthetic column.
species_info_code: Original LEP species code identifying the species name
column2: life stage and
column3: in case of two codes (i.e.
column5: age class of the collected fish. This combination of categorical variables has been resolved into four new separate fields (species_code
column10: and tabulated in the AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv table

column1: fish_length_mm: The total length of the fish from the tip of the snout to the fork/end of the tail in millimeters (i.e.
column2: fork length).Note that most often
column3: the length was assumed not to change due to preservative
column4: so there was no length adjustment. Some records had their values adjusted in case of typos or unit conversion (e.g.
column6: with flagging of any changes that were made to the raw data.

column1: fish_weight_g: The total weight of the fish in grams
column2: including preservative content. Some records had their values adjusted in case of typos or unit conversion (e.g.
column3: mg to g). They were flagged in the "data_issues" column as length_weight_comment. Raw column
column4: with flagging of any changes that were made to the raw data.

column1: orig_std_weight_g: Standardized fish weight in grams (as reported in the source files)
column2: calculated using the provided weight conversion formula based on the preservative in which the specimen was stored.

column1: Weight conversion formulas can be found in the look up tables in the folder *00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table*.
Use cautiously: orig_std_weight_g may contain either fish_weight_g or preservation-adjusted weights
column2: as some values are inconsistent with recalculated corrections.

column1: Raw column.
calc_std_weight_g: This value is a re-estimate of the imported “orig_std_weight_g” value
column2: calculated using the observed weight
column3: fish_weight_g
column4: and the weight conversion formula based on the preservative in which the specimen was stored

column1: Weights are reported in grams.
44 values differ from the original given standardized weight (orig_std_weight_g)
column2: indicating potential inconsistencies in the original column.

column1: These were flagged in the data_issues column as a std_weight_g_comment.
Weight conversion formulas can be found in the look up table 00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table.
A total of 29
column2: 875 standardized weights could not be calculated because the preservative information was missing.

column1: They were flagged in the "data_validation_comments" column as a std_weight_g_comment.
Synthetic column.
calc_K_factor: Calculated K factor using the following formula: length_cm = fish_length_mm / 10
column2: K = 100 * calc_std_weight_g / (length_cm^3). Provided for analysis and data validation purposes (e.g. identification of outliers).

column1: preservative_code: Code identifying the preservative method with which the specimen was stored. An additional preservative code (5) was added to the lookup table (00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table) for 95% Ethanol. This information derived from the trawl_comment (originally called trawl_location). If the search term “Ethanol*” was found in the trawl_comment column
column2: the preservative code and description info was extracted from it. Some records had their values corrected in case of typos (e.g.
column4: 70% etoh; 35/350 = code 3

column1: preservative_description: Description of the preservation technique used to store the specimen. Synthetic column created using the preservative_code and a lookup table (00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table).

weight_conversion_formula: Formula to calculate standardized weight depending on the preservative used. Synthetic column created using the preservative_code and a lookup table (00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table).
age_class: Synthetic column applicable only to Sockeye salmon based on subjective age class identification
column2: by observer
column3: in input field species_code_information (the outdated LEP “species code” that mixes species
column4: life_stage
column5: and age information). Specifically applies to species_code_information = 16 (Sockeye juvenile Age 1) and species_code_information = 17 (Sockeye juvenile Age 2) in the original look-up table. See 00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv table.

column1: aging_technique: Numerical code to identify the technique used to determine fish age; only 1 and 5 are used here
column2: as those were the only codes in the original trawl data. Raw column.

column1: aging_technique_name: Name of the technique referent to the code used to determine fish age in the aging_technique column: (1) “Scale & aged”
column2: (5) “Defaulted.” It is unclear what “Defaulted” means. It is also unclear where in the historical files to find the age data that correspond to aging_technique = 1. Synthetic column created using the aging_technique and a lookup table:code 0 = No scale.code 1 = Scale & aged.code 2 = Scale - Poor.code 3 = Scale - Regen.code 4 = Scale - Unsure.code 5 = Defaulted.

column1: sas_scale: Scale number extracted from original .sas file only
column2: and ranges from 0 to 20.

column1: The code “0” means no scale
column2: and numbers 1-20 presumably indicate the numbers within a specific scale book
column3: but we have so far been unable to match these to ageing data in our archives.

column1: Standard scale book types house 1-25 or 1-50 (MacLellan 2004).
Any "999" code was replaced by NA.
It is uncertain what this variable represents.

column1: Raw column.
sas_scale_book: Alphabetic code

dat_scale_book: These numbers range from 1-110

dat_scale_book_letter: Alphabetic code; It is uncertain what this variable represents.

column1: These are raw values extracted from the original \*.dat files.

general_comments: Concatenation of columns with general comments from original files. It is organized as ["name of the original/intermediate column": "comment"]. Note that the original *.dat files did not have column names
column2: so these were assigned; see appendix for the names assigned to those fields. Also note that HS or HSTIFF (Howard Stiff) was involved in converting the *.dat data into SAS data in past years
column3: so references to him in the general_comments (which come directly from the input files) do not indicate a data issue or change that was made as part of the current (Alice Assmar) data recovery and cleaning.

column1: trawl_comment: Formerly named "trawl_location" in the intermediate *.dat data
column2: it includes unprocessed general trawl location information
column3: as well as additional information on preservative
column4: processor

column1: sas_comment: This was a general comment included from the SAS input files.
trawl_number_comment: Unprocessed general comments associated with trawl numbers in the original files. These comments were extracted from the trawl_number column in *.dat and stored in this field.
trawl_date_comment: Unprocessed general comments associated with trawl dates in the original files. These comments were extracted from the trawl_date column and stored in this field.

time_comment: Unprocessed general comments associated with start-time and end-time in the original files. These comments were extracted from the orig_start_time and orig_end_time columns and stored in this field.
depth_m_comments: Unprocessed general comments related to depth in the original files. These comments were extracted from the depth_m column and stored in this field.

preservative_code_comment: Unprocessed general comments associated with preservative code in the original files. These comments were extracted from the preservative_code column and stored in this field.
preservative_note: Unprocessed general comments associated with the preservative code in the lookup table 00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_DFO_preservative_code_lookup_table.
scale_book_comment: Unprocessed general comments associated with the scale book field in the original files. These comments were extracted from the scale_book column and stored in this field.

data_issues: Concatenation of errors or modifications identified during the data validation step. Some of these comment categories are also used in the data_validation_comments column; this was because no changes
column2: errors or conflicts occurred or were detected in the record
column3: therefore the comment was included in the data_validation instead of in the data_issues column. Organized as ["name of original/intermediate column": "comment"].

column1: duration_comment: Flags errors in the orig_duration_minutes field. The comment "does NOT match calculated orig_start_time and orig_end_time
column2: likely end_time error" indicates that the duration calculated from the orig_start_time and orig_end_time fields differs from the original orig_duration_minutes value.

column1: length_weight_comment: Flags anomalies in the fish_length_mm and fish_weight_g fields. Indicates modifications applied to values in these fields during validation
column2: or comments such as “Unusual size” that was determined when the K factor was between 2-5.

column1: duplicate_flag: Flags records with identical fish_unique_ID values after the merging process. These records are likely duplicates but contain conflicting information in one or more fields
column2: particularly life_stage.

column1: no_species_name_comments: Flags records with missing or unresolved species names. These cases may correspond to trawls in which no fish were collected or to records where species identification is absent or incomplete in the original data.

gear_type_comment: Flags records with inconsistent net dimensions or those suspected to be test trawls; this column also flags when the gear type was determined from a comments field in the input data.
lake_comment: Flags modifications in the lake name
column2: when the lake code from No Name Lake was changed to Link Lake.

column1: taxonomic_comment: Flags possible taxonomic issue with species name.
scale_book_issues: Flags modifications in the scale_book field.

std_weight_g_comment: Flags errors in the orig_std_weight_g field. The comment "does NOT match calc_std_weight_g" indicates that the recalculated standardized weight (using the preservative formula) differs from the original orig_std_weight_g value.
calc_end_time_comment: Flags when orig_end_time was not present in the original file. The comment "orig_end_time missing
column2: calculated from orig_start_time + orig_duration_minutes" indicates that calc_end_time was synthetically calculated from orig_start_time and orig_duration_minutes.

column1: test_trawl_comment: Indicates a suspected test/dummy trawl record. The associated orig_start_time
column2: depth_m
column3: orig_duration_minutes
column4: and trawl_number are artificial placeholders and have been set to NA. Comment: "Fake orig_start_time
column6: orig_duration_minutes and trawl_number. Set to NA."

column1: start_time_comment: Indicates that orig_start_time was missing and has been estimated using the time interval defined by the preceding and following trawls on the same date (i.e.
column2: interpolated between adjacent sequential trawls).

column1: invalid_duration_time: Flags anomalies in the orig_duration_minutes field.
invalid_start_time: Flags records with invalid time formats in the orig_start_time (e.g.
column2: hours outside 0–24
column3: or minutes/seconds outside 0–60).

column1: invalid_end_time: Flags records with invalid time formats in the orig_end_time (e.g.
column2: hours outside 0–24
column3: or minutes/seconds outside 0–60).

column1: preservative_code_issue: Flags modifications in the preservative_code field.
age_class_flag: Indicates records originally coded as 0
column2: which are likely errors. These values have been set to NA in the dataset.

column1: source_files: Name of the original file that the information was extracted from. Synthetic column.
source_line: This is a numerical value indicating the specific line number of this record in the original source file (*.dat only
column2: not SAS files). Synthetic column.

column1: trawl_unique_ID: Unique identifier for trawls created using the following columns: Trawl_date | Lake_code | Trawl_number | Depth_m. Synthetic column.

fish_unique_ID: Unique identifier for each fish created using the following columns: Trawl_date | Lake_code | Trawl_number | Depth_m | Species_info_code | Fish_id | weight_g | Length_mm. Synthetic column. Left as it was in the original
column2: even after correction in fish length and weight.

column1: genus_name: Scientific genus name of the fish
column2: from species_code
column3: based on the AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv table. Synthetic column.

column1: species_name: Scientific specific name of the fish
column2: from species_code
column3: based on the AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv table. Synthetic column.

column1: lake_latitude_decimal: Latitudinal geographical coordinates converted to decimal degrees as per modern convention. Synthetic column created using the lake_code and a lookup table (LDP_ATS_Rescue/TRAWL_BIOSAMPLE/00_raw_data/04_YS_look_up_tables/lake_codes.csv).

lake_longitude_decimal: Longitudinal geographical coordinates converted to decimal degrees as per modern convention. Synthetic column created using the lake_code and a lookup table (LDP_ATS_Rescue/TRAWL_BIOSAMPLE/00_raw_data/04_YS_look_up_tables/lake_codes.csv).
processor: Code identifying the researcher who entered the trawl record into the database. Researcher names are not disclosed for privacy reasons. Raw column.
process_date: The day the record was entered into the database. ISO Formatted in YYY-MM-DD. Raw column.
fish_class_total: Integer identifying the total number of fish within a sample (formerly called fish_total). Raw column.
sample_number: This field is present in some *.sas-formated files (i.e.
column2: as "sample" "Sample No" without the underline) and it is unclear what it represents. Raw column.

column1: program_notes: LDP internship information and time stamp when the data was recovered. Synthetic column.
### Data issues explanation
Some duplicated rows remain in the final dataset. These records can be verified in detail in the duplicated_df_trawl.csv table. n = 56.
There are 146 records without common fish species names. These records can be also found in detail in the 03_errors_out/no_species_record_rows.csv table.
Codes 9 or 99 usually represent "unknown" data
column2: except for "preservative_code" column
column3: which used codes 9 and 98 instead
column4: to indicate unknowns. All of these were replaced by NAs in the final dataset.

column1: In total
column3: 029 and 928 invalid formats were detected in the orig_start_time and orig_end_time columns
column4: respectively
column5: and flagged in the "data_issue" column. They can be also found in the errors tables (03_errors_out/orig_end_time_errors.csv and 03_errors_out/orig_start_time_errors.csv). They were replaced by NAs in the final dataset.

column1: 351 preservative descriptions information that were nested in the trawl_comment column were transferred to the preservative_description column. They were located using the grep command using the pattern "ethanol".
In total
column3: 163 invalid formats were detected in the orig_duration_minutes column and flagged in the "data_issues" column.

column1: Seine information nested in the trawl_comment column was copied to the gear_type column using the grep command to search for the pattern "sein".

The original "species_code" was renamed as "species_info_code" and included species and life stage information. The new "species_code" column is a standardization of codes to reflect only the species name. Detailed information can be found in the AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv look up table
column2: located at 00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables.

column1: Kokanee corresponded to species_info_code 32 in the dataset. This code does not exist in original look up tables
column2: only code 25 existed for this species (00_raw_data/04_YS_look_up_tables/fish_species_code_lookup_table.csv); therefore
column3: code 32 was included in a new look up table as unknown life stage of Kokanee (00_raw_data/03_AA_look_up_tables/AA_fish_scientific_name_lookup.csv). Note that in some lakes
column4: juvenile kokanee are present but cannot be distinguished from juvenile Sockeye; kokanee may therefore have been misattributed as Sockeye. The analyst should check available information for the possible presence of kokanee in specific lakes. Kokanee and Sockeye would not have been distinguished by means of DNA analysis at that time.
